laws.title: Michigan Minimum Wage — $13.73/hr (Effective January 1, 2026)
laws.summary: Michigan's minimum wage increased to $13.73/hour effective January 1, 2026, under the Improved Workforce Opportunity Wage Act as restored by the Michigan Supreme Court. The next scheduled increase is to $15.00/hour on January 1, 2027. Tipped employees must receive at least $5.49/hour (40% of minimum wage). Employers should update payroll systems and post the updated required wage notice.
compliance_actions: Update payroll to pay at least $13.73/hour for all non-exempt employees
Update payroll to pay tipped employees at least $5.49/hour
Post updated Michigan Wage and Hour posters in the workplace
Prepare for next increase to $15.00/hour on January 1, 2027
laws.source: Michigan Department of Labor and Economic Opportunity
